args.content:
# Replication Package: Analysis of the Anthropic Economic Index v5

## Overview

This replication package accompanies an analysis of the Anthropic Economic Index (AEI) v5 (Massenkoff et al., 2026). The AEI data covers one week of Claude.ai usage (2026-02-05 to 2026-02-12), mapping user requests to O\*NET 30.1 occupational task statements. The package constructs a star-schema database of task-level usage, success rates, time estimates, and geographic breakdowns, then derives coding/non-coding classifications and computes the share of non-coding requests by country.

Two Stata scripts in `code/` run the analysis: the first classifies tasks as coding or non-coding using a hand-curated BTOS crosswalk; the second computes and plots the share of non-coding requests by country. A validation script (`test_geo_subset.do`) confirms that country-state data is a proper subset of country-level data. The full pipeline (including data download, normalization, and rollups) can be reproduced via `make`, which requires DuckDB, Python, and Stata. Total runtime is under 10 minutes on a standard desktop machine.

## Data Availability and Provenance Statements

### Anthropic Economic Index (AEI) v5

The AEI raw data are published by Anthropic under a CC-BY 4.0 license (Anthropic, 2026). The `.csv` files were downloaded from https://huggingface.co/datasets/Anthropic/EconomicIndex/tree/main/release_2026_03_24/data. The upstream documentation is mirrored in this package as `data/raw/data_documentation.md`. This is the fifth AEI release (2026-03-24). Two raw long-format files are consumed:

| Raw file | Platform | Geography |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| `aei_raw_claude_ai_2026-02-05_to_2026-02-12.csv` | Claude AI (Free and Pro) | global, country, country-state |
| `aei_raw_1p_api_2026-02-05_to_2026-02-12.csv` | 1P API | global only |

Each raw row is one `(geo_id, date, facet, variable, cluster_name, value)` tuple. The 1P API release is "based on a sample of 1P API traffic and analyzed using privacy-preserving methods" and has no geographic breakdown.

### O\*NET 30.1 Database

The task dimension is joined to official O\*NET 30.1 task statements, distributed as a `bead` input under `input/onet-30.1/` (sourced from the National Center for O\*NET Development; CC-BY 4.0; see `input/onet-30.1/README.md`). Run `bead input load onet-30.1` if `input/onet-30.1/` is empty.

### BTOS Crosswalk

The `output/dim/iwa_btos_crosswalk.csv` file is an original mapping created for this project, linking 332 O\*NET Intermediate Work Activities to 10 Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTOS) activity categories. Category 7 ("Software coding or debugging") is used to flag coding tasks.

### Statement about Rights

- [x] I certify that the author(s) of the manuscript have legitimate access to and permission to use the data used in this manuscript.
- [x] I certify that the author(s) of the manuscript have documented permission to redistribute/publish the data contained within this replication package. Appropriate permissions are documented in the [LICENSE.txt](LICENSE.txt) file.

### License for Data

The AEI raw data are licensed under CC-BY 4.0 (Anthropic, 2026). The O\*NET 30.1 data are licensed under CC-BY 4.0 (National Center for O\*NET Development, 2024). The BTOS crosswalk (`output/dim/iwa_btos_crosswalk.csv`) is an original mapping created for this project. See [LICENSE.txt](LICENSE.txt) for details.

### Summary of Availability

- [x] All data **are** publicly available.

### Details on Each Data Source

| Data Name | Data Files | Location | Provided | Citation |
|-----------|-----------|----------|----------|----------|
| AEI raw Claude AI usage | `aei_raw_claude_ai_2026-02-05_to_2026-02-12.csv` | `temp/` (downloaded by `make`) | Yes | Anthropic (2026) |
| AEI raw 1P API usage | `aei_raw_1p_api_2026-02-05_to_2026-02-12.csv` | `temp/` (downloaded by `make`) | Yes | Anthropic (2026) |
| O\*NET 30.1 task statements | `task_statements.parquet` | `input/onet-30.1/` | Yes | O\*NET (2024) |
| O\*NET 30.1 tasks-to-DWAs | `tasks_to_dwas.parquet` | `input/onet-30.1/` | Yes | O\*NET (2024) |
| O\*NET 30.1 DWA reference | `dwa_reference.parquet` | `input/onet-30.1/` | Yes | O\*NET (2024) |
| O\*NET 30.1 IWA reference | `iwa_reference.parquet` | `input/onet-30.1/` | Yes | O\*NET (2024) |
| O\*NET 30.1 content model | `content_model_reference.parquet` | `input/onet-30.1/` | Yes | O\*NET (2024) |
| BTOS crosswalk | `iwa_btos_crosswalk.csv` | `output/dim/` | Yes | This project |

## Dataset List

| Data file | Source | Notes | Provided |
|-----------|--------|-------|----------|
| `output/dim/task.csv` | AEI + O\*NET 30.1 | 3,395 task statements matched to O\*NET | Yes |
| `output/dim/dwa.csv` | O\*NET 30.1 | 2,087 Detailed Work Activities | Yes |
| `output/dim/iwa.csv` | O\*NET 30.1 | 332 Intermediate Work Activities | Yes |
| `output/dim/element.csv` | O\*NET 30.1 | 37 content-model elements | Yes |
| `output/dim/geo.csv` | AEI | 1,435 geographies | Yes |
| `output/dim/task_unmatched.csv` | AEI | 247 unmatched task texts | Yes |
| `output/dim/iwa_btos_crosswalk.csv` | This project | 333 IWA-to-BTOS links | Yes |
| `output/fct/request_detail.csv` | AEI + O\*NET | 5,176 global task-level rows | Yes |
| `output/fct/request_geo.csv` | AEI + O\*NET | 13,867 per-geography task rows | Yes |
| `output/fct/iwa_detail.csv` | Derived | 332 IWA-level rows | Yes |
| `output/fct/element_detail.csv` | Derived | 37 element-level rows | Yes |
| `output/fct/btos_detail.csv` | Derived | 10 BTOS-category rows | Yes |
| `data/derived/task_coding_crosswalk.csv` | Derived | 3,395 tasks with coding flag (0/1) | Yes |
| `data/derived/iwa_coding_crosswalk.csv` | Derived | 9 IWAs flagged as coding (BTOS 7) | Yes |
| `data/derived/noncoding_by_country.csv` | Derived | 117 countries with noncoding share | Yes |
| `output/noncoding_by_country.csv` | Derived | 118 countries with noncoding share | Yes |
| `output/fig/noncoding_by_country.pdf` | Derived | Bar chart of top 20 countries | Yes |
| `output/element_success_speedup.pdf` | Derived | Element-level figure | Yes |
| `output/btos_success_speedup.pdf` | Derived | BTOS-level figure | Yes |

## Computational Requirements

### Software Requirements

- GNU Make
- Stata 18.0 MP (code was last run with version 18 MP; required for `code/*.do` scripts)
- DuckDB 1.4.4+ (command-line interface; required for SQL scripts in the full pipeline)
- Python 3.11+ (managed via `uv`; required for plot scripts)
  - `matplotlib` (installed automatically by `uv run`)
- `curl` (for downloading raw data from HuggingFace)
- `bead` (for loading O\*NET input data)

No `requirements.txt` or install script is needed for Python; `uv run` handles Python dependencies automatically via PEP 723 inline metadata in each `.py` script.

### Controlled Randomness

- No pseudo-random number generator is used in the analysis described here.

### Memory, Runtime, Storage Requirements

#### Summary

- Approximate time needed to reproduce the analyses: **<10 minutes**
- Approximate storage space needed: **25 MB -- 250 MB**

#### Details

The code was last run on a macOS machine. The full `make` pipeline takes approximately 10--30 seconds (excluding data download time). The raw CSV files (~150 MB total) are downloaded from HuggingFace on the first run. O\*NET Parquet files (~20 MB) are loaded via `bead input load onet-30.1`. Stata scripts in `code/` each run in under 10 seconds.

## Description of Programs/code

### Full pipeline (via `make`)

- `Makefile` -- Orchestrates the full pipeline: downloads raw data, runs all SQL scripts, generates figures, and copies documentation.
- `normalize.sql` -- Main processing script. Joins AEI task strings to O\*NET 30.1 `task_statements` (exact match on a normalised key; residuals fall back to Levenshtein fuzzy match within 25% of the longer string, blocked on the first three normalised characters). Pivots the long format to one row per task, splits global vs. per-geography facts, and writes all dimension and fact tables.
- `iwa_rollup.sql` -- Aggregates task-level facts into IWA-level facts via the DWA hierarchy.
- `element_rollup.sql` -- Aggregates IWA-level facts into element-level facts (unweighted).
- `btos_rollup.sql` -- Aggregates IWA-level facts into BTOS activity categories using `output/dim/iwa_btos_crosswalk.csv` (unweighted).
- `plot_element.py` -- Generates `output/element_success_speedup.pdf` (twin horizontal bar chart of success rate and AI speedup by element).
- `plot_btos.py` -- Generates `output/btos_success_speedup.pdf` (twin horizontal bar chart of success rate and AI speedup by BTOS category).

### Stata analysis scripts (in `code/`)

- `classify_iwa.do` -- Classifies IWAs as coding vs. non-coding. Reads `dim/iwa_btos_crosswalk.csv`, keeps only BTOS category 7 ("Software coding or debugging"). Outputs `data/derived/iwa_coding_crosswalk.csv` (9 IWAs flagged as coding).
- `classify_tasks.do` -- Applies coding classification to tasks via the DWA hierarchy. Reads `dim/task.csv`, extracts `iwa_id` from `dwa_id` by stripping the D-suffix, merges with `iwa_coding_crosswalk.csv` (left join; unmatched tasks get coding=0). Outputs `data/derived/task_coding_crosswalk.csv` (179 coding, 3,216 non-coding).
- `noncoding_by_country.do` -- Computes share of non-coding requests by country. Reads `fct/request_geo.csv`, merges coding flags, filters to country-level, aggregates total and non-coding requests per country. Outputs `output/noncoding_by_country.csv` (118 rows).
- `plot_noncoding_by_country.do` -- Generates horizontal bar chart of top 20 countries by non-coding share. Outputs `output/fig/noncoding_by_country.pdf`.
- `test_geo_subset.do` -- Validation script. Three tests: (a) every country-state task also appears at country level, (b) no region exceeds country count, (c) sum of regions <= country count (+1 rounding tolerance). All 3 tests PASS: 6,944 region-task pairs verified.

### License for Code

The code is licensed under the MIT License. See [LICENSE.txt](LICENSE.txt) for details.

## Instructions to Replicators

### Option A: Full pipeline via `make`

1. Ensure DuckDB (>=1.4.4), Python (>=3.11), `uv`, `bead`, `curl`, and GNU Make are installed and on your `PATH`.
2. Run `make` from the root of this directory. This will:
   - Download the two AEI raw CSV files from HuggingFace to `temp/`.
   - Load O\*NET 30.1 Parquet files via `bead input load onet-30.1` (if not already present).
   - Run `normalize.sql` to produce all dimension and fact tables in `output/`.
   - Run rollup SQL scripts to produce IWA, element, and BTOS fact tables.
   - Run Python plot scripts to produce element and BTOS figures.
   - Copy `README.md` and `data_documentation.md` to `output/`.
3. All outputs are written to `output/` and temporary files to `temp/`.

### Option B: Stata analysis scripts only

1. Ensure Stata 18.0 MP (or compatible version) is installed.
2. Run the following from the `code/` directory:
   ```
   /Applications/Stata/StataMP.app/Contents/MacOS/stata-mp -q -b do classify_iwa.do
   /Applications/Stata/StataMP.app/Contents/MacOS/stata-mp -q -b do classify_tasks.do
   /Applications/Stata/StataMP.app/Contents/MacOS/stata-mp -q -b do noncoding_by_country.do
   /Applications/Stata/StataMP.app/Contents/MacOS/stata-mp -q -b do plot_noncoding_by_country.do
   ```
3. Run the validation script to verify data integrity:
   ```
   /Applications/Stata/StataMP.app/Contents/MacOS/stata-mp -q -b do test_geo_subset.do
   ```
4. Outputs are written to `data/derived/` and `output/`.

## List of Tables and Programs

| Output | Program | Note |
|--------|---------|------|
| `output/dim/task.csv` | `normalize.sql` | |
| `output/dim/task_unmatched.csv` | `normalize.sql` | |
| `output/dim/geo.csv` | `normalize.sql` | |
| `output/dim/dwa.csv` | `normalize.sql` | Pass-through from O\*NET |
| `output/dim/iwa.csv` | `normalize.sql` | Pass-through from O\*NET |
| `output/dim/element.csv` | `normalize.sql` | Pass-through from O\*NET |
| `output/dim/iwa_btos_crosswalk.csv` | Hand-curated | IWA-to-BTOS mapping |
| `output/fct/request_detail.csv` | `normalize.sql` | |
| `output/fct/request_geo.csv` | `normalize.sql` | |
| `output/fct/iwa_detail.csv` | `iwa_rollup.sql` | |
| `output/fct/element_detail.csv` | `element_rollup.sql` | |
| `output/fct/btos_detail.csv` | `btos_rollup.sql` | |
| `data/derived/iwa_coding_crosswalk.csv` | `code/classify_iwa.do` | 9 coding IWAs |
| `data/derived/task_coding_crosswalk.csv` | `code/classify_tasks.do` | 3,395 tasks with coding flag |
| `data/derived/noncoding_by_country.csv` | `code/noncoding_by_country.do` | 117 countries |
| `output/noncoding_by_country.csv` | `code/noncoding_by_country.do` | 118 countries (includes NONE) |
| `output/fig/noncoding_by_country.pdf` | `code/plot_noncoding_by_country.do` | Top 20 countries bar chart |
| `output/element_success_speedup.pdf` | `plot_element.py` | |
| `output/btos_success_speedup.pdf` | `plot_btos.py` | |

## Schema

### Question

What do people use Claude for? Do these requests succeed? Where are users located?

### Grain

Weekly summary, no individual requests. The release covers one week, 2026-02-05 to 2026-02-12. The data is organised as a small star schema with two fact tables and two dimension tables.

### Dimensions

- `output/dim/task.csv` -- the subset of O\*NET 30.1 task statements that match an AEI task. 3,395 rows, one per `task_id`. Columns:
  - `task_id` -- official O\*NET task id (integer, range 1--24,013).
  - `onetsoc_code` -- O\*NET-SOC occupation the task belongs to (e.g. `11-1011.00`).
  - `task_name` -- canonical O\*NET task statement.
  - `dwa_id` -- Detailed Work Activity the task maps to. The relationship is **not** 1:1 -- 23% of tasks link to multiple DWAs (sourced from `tasks_to_dwas.parquet`); we keep the smallest `dwa_id` so the dim stays one row per task. Join to `dim/dwa.csv` for the full set.
  - `date_updated` -- `min(date_updated)` of the underlying DWA links.
  - `domain_source` -- provenance of the DWA links.

  Where an AEI task text appears under multiple occupations in O\*NET, we keep the row with the smallest `task_id`.
- `output/dim/dwa.csv` -- pass-through of O\*NET 30.1 `dwa_reference`: 2,087 Detailed Work Activities with `element_id`, `iwa_id`, `dwa_id`, `dwa_title`.
- `output/dim/iwa.csv` -- pass-through of O\*NET 30.1 `iwa_reference`: Intermediate Work Activities with `element_id`, `iwa_id`, `iwa_title`.
- `output/dim/element.csv` -- pass-through of O\*NET 30.1 `content_model_reference`: the hierarchy of O\*NET content-model nodes with `element_id`, `element_name`, `description`.
- `output/dim/task_unmatched.csv` -- 247 AEI task texts that could not be matched to any O\*NET 30.1 statement within the 25% Levenshtein threshold (4% of all distinct AEI tasks).
- `output/dim/geo.csv` -- `geo_id` and its level of geography. 1,435 rows: 1 `global`, 178 `country` (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes such as `US`, `GB`), and 1,256 `country-state` (ISO 3166-2 codes such as `US-CA`, `AGO-LUA`).
- `output/dim/iwa_btos_crosswalk.csv` -- mapping from O\*NET 30.1 Intermediate Work Activities to BTOS activity categories. 333 rows covering 10 categories.

### Fact tables

- `output/fct/request_detail.csv` -- 5,176 rows, one per (`date_start`, `date_end`, `source`, `task`). Global aggregate only. Contains `n_requests`, `pct_successful` (null for `claude` source), token/cost indices (API source only), `human_time` and `ai_time` in minutes.

  > **Unit caveat.** The upstream release stores `human_only_time` in **hours** and `human_with_ai_time` in **minutes** -- a unit mismatch not flagged in `data_documentation.md`. `normalize.sql` multiplies `human_only_time` by 60 so that both columns are expressed in minutes.

- `output/fct/request_geo.csv` -- 13,867 rows, one per (`geo_id`, `date_start`, `date_end`, `task`). Per-geography task counts only (no success/time/cost breakdown by geo). Covers 478 geographies.
- `output/fct/iwa_detail.csv` -- 332 rows, one per IWA. Rolled up from tasks via DWA hierarchy.
- `output/fct/element_detail.csv` -- 37 rows, one per element. Rolled up from IWAs.
- `output/fct/btos_detail.csv` -- 10 rows, one per BTOS category. Rolled up from IWAs via the hand-curated crosswalk.

### Derived outputs

- `data/derived/iwa_coding_crosswalk.csv` -- 9 IWAs flagged as coding (BTOS category 7).
- `data/derived/task_coding_crosswalk.csv` -- 3,395 tasks with coding flag (179 = coding, 3,216 = non-coding).
- `data/derived/noncoding_by_country.csv` -- 117 countries with share of non-coding requests.
- `output/noncoding_by_country.csv` -- 118 countries (includes `NONE` = not_classified).
- `output/fig/noncoding_by_country.pdf` -- horizontal bar chart, top 20 countries by non-coding share.
- `output/element_success_speedup.pdf` -- twin horizontal bar chart of success rate and AI speedup by element.
- `output/btos_success_speedup.pdf` -- twin horizontal bar chart of success rate and AI speedup by BTOS category.

### Geography

- Country codes: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g., `US`, `GB`, `HU`)
- Country-state codes: ISO 3166-2 (e.g., `US-CA`, `HU-BU`)
- `NONE` / `not_classified`: filtered for privacy, not a real geography
- Thresholds: >=200 conversations for country, >=100 for country-state

## References

Anthropic. 2026. "Anthropic Economic Index v5." https://huggingface.co/datasets/Anthropic/EconomicIndex

Massenkoff, Maxim, Eva Lyubich, Peter McCrory, Ruth Appel, and Ryan Heller. 2026. "Anthropic Economic Index report: Learning curves." https://www.anthropic.com/research/economic-index-march-2026-report

Appel, Ruth, Maxim Massenkoff, Peter McCrory, Miles McCain, Ryan Heller, Tyler Neylon, and Alex Tamkin. 2026. "Economic Primitives." Chapter 2 in *Anthropic Economic Index report: Learning curves*. https://www.anthropic.com/research/economic-index-march-2026-report

National Center for O\*NET Development. 2024. "O\*NET 30.1 Database." O\*NET Resource Center. https://www.onetcenter.org/database.html. Sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor, Employment and Training Administration.

messages.content:
Created `README.md` at the project root following the template structure. It includes all required sections:

- **Overview** -- summary of the replication package
- **Data Availability and Provenance Statements** -- AEI, O\*NET, and BTOS crosswalk sources
- **Statement about Rights** and **License for Data**
- **Summary of Availability** -- all data publicly available
- **Details on Each Data Source** -- table with file locations and citations
- **Dataset List** -- all provided files
- **Computational Requirements** -- software, runtime, storage
- **Description of Programs/code** -- both the full `make` pipeline and the Stata scripts
- **Instructions to Replicators** -- two options (full pipeline or Stata-only)
- **List of Tables and Programs** -- mapping of outputs to programs
- **Schema** -- dimensions, facts, derived outputs, geography
- **References** -- Anthropic, Massenkoff et al., Appel et al., O\*NET
messages.thinking: The README has been written successfully. Let me verify it was written correctly by reading it back.
